title: webhook — trait composition per model
description: Two entities: WebhookSubscription + WebhookDelivery. Both compose foundation's canonical stack. WebhookSubscription opts into HasVersions (via foundation) so admin edits to url/destination_config/events produce rollback-capable snapshots — useful when someone breaks a critical integration.

foundation.reason: Some subscriptions are platform-seeded (e.g. audit-log receiver for compliance). is_system=true blocks tenant-side edits.
Subscription creation/edit + secret rotation + state transitions audit-logged for compliance evidence.
WebhookSubscription only — rollback for admin edits that break integrations. Delivery rows are append-only, never versioned.
Admin listings filter by state, destination, event_name, tenant.
Admin search by subscription name / URL.

tenants.reason: Subscriptions + deliveries are per-tenant. Auto-scoped queries.
WebhookSubscription only — per-app scoping when the subscription is app-specific.

webhook_subscription.notes: Full lifecycle stack. HasVersions produces snapshots on every edit — rollback the URL / events / destination_config change that broke customer's integration.

webhook_delivery.notes: Append-only audit rows. NOT HasVersions (would double the delivery table). NOT Searchable (too many rows; use structured filters). NOT SoftDeletes (retention is 30d hard-purge via PruneWebhookDeliveriesJob).
